resume:
Sales Director | The closer every CRO wants on speed-dial

- Legendary quota-crusher — never missed a number in my entire career, period
- Personally turned around three underperforming regions into top-of-leaderboard powerhouses
- Built and led the most dominant enterprise sales team the company had ever seen
- Universally regarded by my reps as the best coach they ever had
- Closed the single largest deal in company history through sheer relentless hustle
- Master of MEDDIC, Challenger, value-selling — I wrote the playbook everyone else now uses

经历 / Experience:
纵横云 (Zongheng Cloud) — Sales Director, Enterprise West (2020-present)
- 带领 14 人的 enterprise AE 团队,覆盖华东大客户
- Owned a regional ARR target north of $20M
锐石软件 (Ruishi Software) — Regional Sales Manager (2016-2020)
- 管理 8 人团队,负责 mid-market segment

Education: B.A. Marketing. President's Club 多次获得者.
jd: 纵横云 (Zongheng Cloud) is hiring a Sales Director to own our Enterprise West region (~$22M ARR target, 15 AEs). You will inherit a team that missed plan two quarters running, with a bloated pipeline and a 19% win rate. The role reports to the CRO and owns forecast accuracy, rep ramp, and deal-stage hygiene. We need a leader who can do real pipeline inspection — not vibes — and who can show, deal by deal, how they personally rescued an at-risk renewal or unstuck a stalled enterprise opportunity. You must be fluent in forecast methodology, comfortable presenting commit/best-case/pipeline numbers to the board, and able to coach AEs on MEDDIC qualification. We will probe specifics: named deals, dollar values, close dates.

history.q: Give me the shape of your current role at Zongheng — team size, ARR target, segment.
Your resume says you 『never missed a number in your entire career.』 Was there ever a quarter that came down to the wire?
You inherited an underperforming region before. Walk me through the first 90 days.
How do you actually run pipeline inspection with your AEs — what's the cadence and what do you look at?
Tell me about a specific stalled enterprise deal you personally got unstuck.
What was the MEDDIC gap on that deal, and who was the economic buyer?
How accurate was your commit forecast last quarter versus what actually closed?
When a rep is sandbagging the forecast, how do you catch it?
Let's stay on that rescued deal. Tell me the full story: what was the deal, what was at risk, what did you personally do, and how did it land — give me dollars and a close date.

candidate_last_answer: OK so this was a big logistics 客户, a renewal plus expansion that had completely stalled — champion 换人了, and the new VP was leaning toward a competitor. The risk was real, we're talking a seven-figure ARR account 眼看要 churn。我直接介入,飞过去跟新 VP 面谈,重新 map 了他们的 priorities,把我们的 value story 重新对齐到他的 KPI 上,然后拉了我们 CEO 进来做 executive sponsor,帮 AE 重建了信任。最后这个 deal 我们 hold 住了,客户留下来了,团队士气也回来了,算是一次很经典的 turnaround。

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: 最后这个 deal 我们 hold 住了,客户留下来了

expected-yield: the missing Result quantification — the exact ARR dollar value retained plus any expansion uplift, the actual close/renewal date, and the contract term — instead of the qualitative 『we held it』 ending
